cases.stolid: showing little emotion or sensitivity, calm and unexcitable
cases.flippant: not showing serious or respectful attention
cases.uncharitable: unfairly harsh or unkind in one's judgment
cases.sleazy: sordid, corrupt, or lacking moral standards
cases.dissuade: to persuade someone not to do something
cases.halcyon: denoting a past period that was idyllically happy and peaceful
cases.unrequited: not returned or reciprocated, especially of love
cases.lascivious: feeling or revealing an overt and often offensive sexual desire
cases.philanthropic: seeking to promote the welfare of others, generous
cases.unwholesome: not conducive to physical, mental, or moral well-being
cases.vindictive: having a strong desire for revenge
cases.onerous: involving a great deal of effort and difficulty, burdensome
cases.caress: to touch or stroke gently and affectionately
cases.brandish: to wave or flourish something, especially a weapon, threateningly
cases.agape: wide open, especially of the mouth in wonder or surprise
cases.impetus: the force or energy that drives something forward, a motivation
cases.swoon: to feel or nearly faint with strong emotion, especially infatuation
cases.rendezvous: a meeting at an agreed time and place, especially a secret one
cases.diatribe: a forceful and bitter verbal attack against someone or something
cases.loquacious: very talkative, tending to chatter a lot
cases.altruism: unselfish concern for the welfare of others
cases.coax: to gently and persistently persuade someone to do something
cases.harp on: to talk or complain about something repeatedly and tediously
cases.incoherent: unclear and difficult to understand, not logically connected
cases.gallant: dignified and courageous, especially in a difficult situation
cases.mystify: to confuse or puzzle someone completely
cases.microcosm: a small representative sample of something larger
cases.pummel: to strike repeatedly with the fists
cases.raucous: making or constituting a disturbingly harsh and loud noise
cases.languid: displaying or having a disinclination for physical exertion, slow and relaxed
cases.fidget: to make small restless movements out of nervousness or impatience
cases.err: to make a mistake, to be incorrect
cases.squander: to waste something, especially time or opportunity, carelessly
cases.blurt: to say something suddenly and without thinking
cases.elusive: difficult to find, catch, or achieve
cases.devoid: entirely lacking or free from something
cases.self-deprecation: the act of criticizing or undervaluing oneself, often humorously
cases.brusque: abrupt or blunt in manner or speech
cases.shameless: showing no shame about something one should be ashamed of
cases.quail: to feel or show fear, to flinch
cases.haggard: looking exhausted and unwell, especially from fatigue
cases.foreboding: a feeling that something bad is about to happen
cases.devout: having or showing deep religious feeling or commitment
cases.flaunt: to display something ostentatiously to attract attention
cases.sinister: giving the impression that something harmful is going to happen, evil-looking
cases.unabashed: not embarrassed or ashamed, open and unapologetic
cases.meager: small in amount and lacking in quality, scanty
cases.capricious: given to sudden and unaccountable changes of mood or behavior
cases.guffaw: a loud and boisterous burst of laughter
cases.timidity: the state of being shy and lacking confidence
cases.dwindle: to gradually diminish in size or amount
cases.despondent: in low spirits from loss of hope or courage
cases.abreast: up to date with or well informed about a subject
cases.unscrupulous: having or showing no moral principles, not honest or fair
cases.melancholy: a deep, pensive, and long-lasting sadness
cases.countermeasure: an action taken to counteract a danger or threat
